The Secretary General ‚Äåof Amnesty International, Agn√®s Callamard, declared that ‚Äúthe wheels of international‚Å§ justice have finally caught up with those allegedly responsible for war crimes and crimes against ‚Äãhumanity committed in Palestine and Israel‚Äù.

The ICC ‚Äçarrest warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant include, unequivocally, charges of war crimes ‚Å£that‚Äã are serious violations of the Geneva Conventions.‚Å§ Every State in the‚Äã world has an obligation to bring to‚Äã justice those who are alleged to have committed ‚Äçthese ‚Äúserious violations‚Äù,‚Äã regardless of the‚Äç nationality of the perpetrator or the victim.
Context On November 21, 2024, Pre-Trial Chamber I of the ICC rendered two ‚Äãunanimous ‚Å¢decisions rejecting appeals initiated by the State of ‚ÄãIsrael (‚ÄúIsrael‚Äù) under Articles‚Äã 18 and 19 of the Rome Statute ‚Äã(the ‚ÄúStatute‚Äù). ‚Äçand issued arrest warrants. for ‚Å¢Benjamin Netanyahu and Yoav Gallant, on charges including the war ‚Äãcrimes of hunger‚Å£ as a means of warfare and for deliberately directing an attack against the civilian population; and crimes against humanity murder, persecution and other inhuman acts.
The same‚Å£ Chamber also issued an‚Å§ arrest warrant against Mohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri (Deif),‚Äã who has been accused since October 7, 2023, of crimes against humanity of murder,‚Äå torture,‚Äç rape and other ‚Å§forms of sexual violence and intentional homicide war crimes. attacks against civilians, hostage-taking, rape and other forms ‚Å¢of sexual‚Äç violence, cruel treatment and‚Å£ conduct of outrages on personal dignity.
The arrest warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant relate to crimes committed between at least ‚ÄçOctober 8, ‚Äç2023 and at‚Å£ least May 20, 2024, the date the‚Äå Prosecutor‚Äôs Office filed the arrest warrant requests. After confirming the‚Äã deaths of ‚ÄçYahya ‚ÄãSinwar‚Å¢ and Ismail Haniyeh,‚Äã the Association approved the withdrawal of the‚Äç applications for arrest warrants against them. As for Deif, ‚Äåthe Chamber noted that it is currently unable to determine whether he‚Äã was killed or ‚Å£whether he is still alive.
The arrest warrants against Netanyahu and Gallant relate to crimes committed between ‚Äåat least October 8, 2023 and ‚Å§at least ‚Å¢May 20, 2024, the date the Prosecutor‚Äôs Office filed requests for arrest warrants.

This‚Å§ decision followed the decision of Pre-Trial Chamber I of the ICC on 5 February 2021 that the Court could exercise its criminal jurisdiction in the State ‚Äåand that the‚Äç territorial ‚Äãscope of that jurisdiction extended to Gaza and the West Bank, including East Jerusalem.

the International Criminal Court‚Äôs issuance of ‚Äåarrest warrants for Benjamin ‚ÄãNetanyahu, Yoav Gallant, and ‚ÄåMohammed Diab Ibrahim Al-Masri represents a ‚Äçsignificant ‚Äådevelopment in the ‚Äåpursuit of accountability for alleged war‚Äã crimes and crimes‚Å§ against humanity in the context of ‚Äãthe Israel-Palestine ‚Äçconflict.‚Å£ Amnesty International ‚Å£has emphasized the importance of ‚Äçthis action in combating the longstanding impunity for human ‚Äårights violations in the region and ‚Äçin restoring faith ‚Äçin international justice systems. ‚Å¢The organization urges all states to respect the ICC‚Äôs decisions and facilitate the‚Å¢ prosecution of those accused of serious international crimes.
